headlines.title: Vets must publish prices and pet prescription fees to be capped at £21, watchdog says
headlines.description: Vets will have to publish price lists for services under measures from the UK's competition watchdog.

headlines.title: Chris Mason: Reeves to set out principles on who might get energy bills support - and who won't
headlines.description: The chancellor must react in real time to the economic shock of the war in Iran, writes the BBC's political editor.

headlines.title: UK's transplant system was world-leading - now it lags behind other Western nations
headlines.description: The UK has failed to keep pace with the rest of the world. Can it regain its status, and how?

headlines.title: Royal Mail staff say they were told to hide post to look like delivery targets met
headlines.description: BBC Your Voice hears from postal workers who say "take the mail for ride" is a common phrase.

headlines.title: 'Like the plane got cut in half': LaGuardia crash that killed two pilots now under investigation
headlines.description: In the aftermath of the collision of Air Canada flight AC8646 with a fire truck in New York, US transportation chief Sean Duffy emphasized that seat belts helped save lives

headlines.title: Police investigating possible Iran link to attack on Jewish charity ambulances
headlines.description: Met Police chief Sir Mark Rowley says they are looking at "an online claim of responsibility by an Islamist group"

headlines.title: All new homes to have heat pumps and plug-in solar
headlines.description: Solar panels that can be plugged in at home could be available to buy in supermarkets in the coming months.

headlines.title: Inside the alleged Russian operation to trigger anti-government protests in Angola
headlines.description: A group of Russians and Angolans are going on trial in Angola after being accused of fomenting protests, which they deny.

headlines.title: Colombian military plane crash kills at least 66
headlines.description: Colombia's defence minister says it was a "tragic accident" but the cause is not yet clear.

headlines.title: Referendum defeat leaves Italy's Meloni looking more vulnerable
headlines.description: Meloni vowed to press on after losing a vote on constitutional reform many turned into a referendum on her government.

headlines.title: Ultimatums, diplomacy and a trip to Graceland as Trump eyes a deal with Iran
headlines.description: America may be a nation at war, but the president's activities have been a mix of diplomacy and diversions - with the occasional swing toward the surreal.

headlines.title: A committed pharmacist and a homesick blogger - the Iranian civilians killed by the war
headlines.description: The toll on civilians is mounting fast as the US-Israeli war on Iran continues.

headlines.title: Oil back above $100 as conflicting claims emerge on US-Iran talks
headlines.description: Global energy prices plunged on Monday after Trump said he had postponed strikes on Iranian power plants.

headlines.title: Could Iranian missiles reach London and Paris?
headlines.description: Some experts say Iran may be able to launch missiles to hit London, but they would likely be shot down long before arriving and the risk is low.

headlines.title: US' and Iran's options for ending war narrow the longer it goes on
headlines.description: Iran shows no sign of climbdown, while air power alone cannot achieve the US' main goal.

headlines.title: Cuba's mothers-to-be prepare to give birth in a country plunged into darkness
headlines.description: Two pregnant women tell the BBC's Will Grant of their hopes and fears as their nation is mired in crisis.

headlines.title: UK universities flock to India - but will they succeed?
headlines.description: Nine UK universities are setting up campuses in India, but enrolment is expected to be modest initially.

headlines.title: Is ex-Google boss Matt Brittin the right person to steer the BBC?
headlines.description: BBC media editor Katie Razzall says the former Google exec has been chosen for his insider knowledge of big tech.

headlines.title: How a YouTuber used a fake live stream to cover up the murder of his pregnant partner
headlines.description: Evidence in the trial of Stephen McCullagh outlined a cold, calculating plan to kill Natalie McNally and get away with it.

headlines.title: The Papers: 'Trump blinks first' and 'horrific' attack on Jewish charity ambulances
headlines.description: Donald Trump's claim of productive talks with Iran and the attack on a Jewish charity in London is the main focus for Tuesday's front pages.

headlines.title: Australia and EU agree sweeping trade deal in face of global uncertainty
headlines.description: Australia and the EU sign sweeping trade and security deals after years of negotiations.

headlines.title: 'Shocking but not unexpected': Jewish community reacts to ambulance attack
headlines.description: Ambulances belonging to a Jewish community charity in north-west London were set on fire on Monday.

headlines.title: UK must back North Sea oil and gas drilling, says trade body
headlines.description: The group says the country "urgently" needs to produce its own oil and gas to secure supplies.

headlines.title: More sightings of the Northern Lights with stunning bright colours
headlines.description: The Northern Lights have been visible in the UK over the last few nights but what makes their distinct bright colours shine in the night sky?

headlines.title: Jury orders Cosby to pay $19m to ex-waitress after finding he abused her in 1972
headlines.description: Donna Motsinger alleged Cosby gave her wine and a pill that left her incapacitated after picking her up in a limousine in 1972.

headlines.title: BBC News app
headlines.description: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

headlines.title: Has Trump Backed Down On Iran Threat?
headlines.description: President Trump says he has postponed strikes on Iranian power plants.

headlines.title: Americanswers… on 5 Live! Trump claims Iran wants to "make a deal" to end the war
headlines.description: Iran denies talks with the US to stop the war as ‘fake news’

headlines.title: McCullum and Key very lucky to survive Ashes review - Vaughan
headlines.description: Former captain Michael Vaughan says England head coach Brendon McCullum and managing director Rob Key were fortunate to avoid the sack in a post-Ashes review.

headlines.title: Football's great entertainers - ranking the biggest showboaters
headlines.description: We all love to watch the players with the flicks and tricks, the mavericks with the magic manoeuvres. Here, BBC Sport ranks football's top showboaters.

headlines.title: Dirt pitches, dodging dogs and choosing Chelsea - the making of Estevao
headlines.description: From the dirt pitch to practising against a rottweiler and Thiago Silva's advice - the foundations of Chelsea forward Estevao's rise.

headlines.title: Has Aston Martin's Newey team principal project failed? F1 Q&A
headlines.description: BBC Sport F1 correspondent Andrew Benson answers your questions before the Japanese Grand Prix.

headlines.title: Is the three-week Premier League break good for your side?
headlines.description: The Premier League is on a break for almost three weeks as international football takes centre stage, but will it give your club a chance to reset or disrupt momentum?

headlines.title: Sexism in football - a problem that isn't going away
headlines.description: BBC Sport speaks to fans, the police and others to examine how common sexism is in football, why it happens, and what can be done.

headlines.title: Cooper accused of dropping Bosnia player before Wales tie
headlines.description: Welsh manager Steve Cooper is accused of dropping a Bosnia-Herzegovina player from Brondby ahead of Wales' World Cup qualifier, a claim his club denies.
